first_paragraph: "**Little Girl Blue**" is a [popular](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music") [song](/wiki/Song "Song") with music by [Richard Rodgers](/wiki/Richard_Rodgers "Richard Rodgers") and lyrics by [Lorenz Hart](/wiki/Lorenz_Hart "Lorenz Hart"), published in 1935.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The song was introduced by Gloria Grafton in the [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theater "Broadway theater") [musical](/wiki/Musical_play "Musical play") *[Jumbo](/wiki/Jumbo_(musical) "Jumbo (musical)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Song

| "Little Girl Blue" | |
| --- | --- |
| [Song](/wiki/Song "Song") | |
| Published | 1935 |
| [Composer](/wiki/Composer "Composer") | [Richard Rodgers](/wiki/Richard_Rodgers "Richard Rodgers") |
| [Lyricist](/wiki/Lyricist "Lyricist") | [Lorenz Hart](/wiki/Lorenz_Hart "Lorenz Hart") |
|  | |
| Audio example | |
| ["Little Girl Blue" by Nina Simone (2013 – Remaster)"](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5t5IJAIkuUs) on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube_video_(identifier) "YouTube video (identifier)") | |
|  | |

"**Little Girl Blue**" is a [popular](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music") [song](/wiki/Song "Song") with music by [Richard Rodgers](/wiki/Richard_Rodgers "Richard Rodgers") and lyrics by [Lorenz Hart](/wiki/Lorenz_Hart "Lorenz Hart"), published in 1935.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The song was introduced by Gloria Grafton in the [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theater "Broadway theater") [musical](/wiki/Musical_play "Musical play") *[Jumbo](/wiki/Jumbo_(musical) "Jumbo (musical)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Film appearances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Little_Girl_Blue_(song)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Film appearances")]

* 1950 *[The Jackpot](/wiki/The_Jackpot "The Jackpot")*
* 1962 *[Billy Rose's Jumbo](/wiki/Billy_Rose%27s_Jumbo "Billy Rose's Jumbo")* – sung by [Doris Day](/wiki/Doris_Day "Doris Day")
* 1990 *[The Handmaid's Tale](/wiki/The_Handmaid%27s_Tale_(film) "The Handmaid's Tale (film)")*
* 2022 *[Saint Omer](/wiki/Saint_Omer_(film) "Saint Omer (film)")* – sung by [Nina Simone](/wiki/Nina_Simone "Nina Simone")

## Recordings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Little_Girl_Blue_(song)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recordings")]

Many popular and jazz artists have recorded the tune, including:

* [The Afghan Whigs](/wiki/The_Afghan_Whigs "The Afghan Whigs") - *Debonair* (1994)
* [Chet Baker](/wiki/Chet_Baker "Chet Baker") - *Chet Baker Meets Space Jazz Trio* (1988)
* [Polly Bergen](/wiki/Polly_Bergen "Polly Bergen") – *Little Girl Blue* (1955)
* [Donald Byrd](/wiki/Donald_Byrd "Donald Byrd") – *[Byrd in Flight](/wiki/Byrd_in_Flight "Byrd in Flight")*, as "Little Boy Blue": (Blue Note 1960)[[3]](#cite_note-3)
* [Ann Hampton Callaway](/wiki/Ann_Hampton_Callaway "Ann Hampton Callaway") – *To Ella with Love* (1996)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* [Barbara Cook](/wiki/Barbara_Cook "Barbara Cook") - *[From The Heart: The Best of Rodgers And Hart](/w/index.php?title=From_The_Heart:_The_Best_of_Rodgers_And_Hart&action=edit&redlink=1 "From The Heart: The Best of Rodgers And Hart (page does not exist)")* (1959)
* [Sam Cooke](/wiki/Sam_Cooke "Sam Cooke") – *[My Kind of Blues](/wiki/My_Kind_of_Blues_(Sam_Cooke_album) "My Kind of Blues (Sam Cooke album)")* (1961)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Doris Day](/wiki/Doris_Day "Doris Day") – *[Billy Rose's Jumbo](/wiki/Billy_Rose%27s_Jumbo_(soundtrack) "Billy Rose's Jumbo (soundtrack)")* (1962)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* [Ethel Ennis](/wiki/Ethel_Ennis "Ethel Ennis") – *Eyes for You* (1964)
* [Ella Fitzgerald](/wiki/Ella_Fitzgerald "Ella Fitzgerald") – *[Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Rodgers & Hart Song Book](/wiki/Ella_Fitzgerald_Sings_the_Rodgers_%26_Hart_Song_Book "Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Rodgers & Hart Song Book")* (1956)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Judy Garland](/wiki/Judy_Garland "Judy Garland") – *[Alone](/wiki/Alone_(Judy_Garland_album) "Alone (Judy Garland album)")* (1957)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* [Red Garland](/wiki/Red_Garland "Red Garland") – *[A Garland of Red](/wiki/A_Garland_of_Red "A Garland of Red")* (Prestige 1956)[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* [The Four Freshmen](/wiki/The_Four_Freshmen "The Four Freshmen") – *[Love Lost](/wiki/Love_Lost_(album) "Love Lost (album)")* (1959)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* [Johnny Hartman](/wiki/Johnny_Hartman "Johnny Hartman") – *[And I Thought About You](/wiki/And_I_Thought_About_You_(Johnny_Hartman_album) "And I Thought About You (Johnny Hartman album)")* (1959)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* [The Hi-Lo's](/wiki/The_Hi-Lo%27s "The Hi-Lo's") - *[Now Hear This](/wiki/Now_Hear_This_(The_Hi-Lo%27s_album) "Now Hear This (The Hi-Lo's album)")*.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* [Milt Jackson](/wiki/Milt_Jackson "Milt Jackson") – *Reverence and Compassion* (1993)[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* [Harry James](/wiki/Harry_James "Harry James")
* [Joni James](/wiki/Joni_James "Joni James") – *Little Girl Blue* (1956)
* [Keith Jarrett](/wiki/Keith_Jarrett "Keith Jarrett") – *[Standards in Norway](/wiki/Standards_in_Norway "Standards in Norway")* (1995)[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* [Janis Joplin](/wiki/Janis_Joplin "Janis Joplin") – *[I Got Dem Ol' Kozmic Blues Again Mama!](/wiki/I_Got_Dem_Ol%27_Kozmic_Blues_Again_Mama! "I Got Dem Ol' Kozmic Blues Again Mama!")* (1969, although the lyrics on this version were rearranged)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* [Morgana King](/wiki/Morgana_King "Morgana King") - *[It's a Quiet Thing](/wiki/It%27s_a_Quiet_Thing "It's a Quiet Thing")* (1965)
* [Diana Krall](/wiki/Diana_Krall "Diana Krall") – *[From This Moment On](/wiki/From_This_Moment_On_(album) "From This Moment On (album)")* (2006)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* [Stacey Kent](/wiki/Stacey_Kent "Stacey Kent") – *[Dreamsville](/wiki/Dreamsville "Dreamsville")* (2001)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* [Brenda Lee](/wiki/Brenda_Lee "Brenda Lee") – *Reflections in Blue* (1964)[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* [Grant Green](/wiki/Grant_Green "Grant Green") – *[Oleo](/wiki/Oleo_(Grant_Green_album) "Oleo (Grant Green album)")* with Sonny Clark (1962)[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* [Eddie Harris](/wiki/Eddie_Harris "Eddie Harris") – *[Exodus to Jazz](/wiki/Exodus_to_Jazz "Exodus to Jazz")* (1961)[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* [John Lewis](/wiki/John_Lewis_(pianist) "John Lewis (pianist)") – *[The John Lewis Piano](/wiki/The_John_Lewis_Piano "The John Lewis Piano")* (1957)[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* [Hank Mobley](/wiki/Hank_Mobley "Hank Mobley") – *[Mobley's Message](/wiki/Mobley%27s_Message "Mobley's Message")* (Prestige 1956)[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* [Gerry Mulligan](/wiki/Gerry_Mulligan "Gerry Mulligan") with [Jon Eardley](/wiki/Jon_Eardley "Jon Eardley") – *[California Concerts](/wiki/California_Concerts "California Concerts")* (1954)[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* [Anita O'Day](/wiki/Anita_O%27Day "Anita O'Day") – *[Anita O'Day and Billy May Swing Rodgers and Hart](/wiki/Anita_O%27Day_and_Billy_May_Swing_Rodgers_and_Hart "Anita O'Day and Billy May Swing Rodgers and Hart")* (1960)[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* [Oscar Peterson](/wiki/Oscar_Peterson "Oscar Peterson") – *[My Favorite Instrument](/wiki/My_Favorite_Instrument "My Favorite Instrument")* (solo piano)[[25]](#cite_note-25)
* [The Postal Service](/wiki/The_Postal_Service "The Postal Service") (a remix of the Nina Simone version) - *[Verve Remixed 3](/wiki/Verve_Remixed "Verve Remixed")* (2005)
* [Sue Raney](/wiki/Sue_Raney "Sue Raney") – *Sue Raney, Volume II* (2004)[[26]](#cite_note-26)
* [Linda Ronstadt](/wiki/Linda_Ronstadt "Linda Ronstadt") – *[For Sentimental Reasons](/wiki/For_Sentimental_Reasons_(Linda_Ronstadt_album) "For Sentimental Reasons (Linda Ronstadt album)")* (1986)[[27]](#cite_note-27)
* [Diana Ross](/wiki/Diana_Ross "Diana Ross") – *[Touch Me in the Morning](/wiki/Touch_Me_in_the_Morning_(album) "Touch Me in the Morning (album)")* (1973)[[28]](#cite_note-28)
* [Mathilde Santing Combo](/wiki/Mathilde_Santing "Mathilde Santing") - *Hand In Hand* (1983)
* [The Carpenters](/wiki/The_Carpenters "The Carpenters") – *[Lovelines](/wiki/Lovelines "Lovelines")* (1989)[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* [Rosemary Clooney](/wiki/Rosemary_Clooney "Rosemary Clooney") – *[Rosemary Clooney Sings Rodgers, Hart & Hammerstein](/wiki/Rosemary_Clooney_Sings_Rodgers,_Hart_%26_Hammerstein "Rosemary Clooney Sings Rodgers, Hart & Hammerstein")* (1990)[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* [Carly Simon](/wiki/Carly_Simon "Carly Simon") – *[My Romance](/wiki/My_Romance_(Carly_Simon_album) "My Romance (Carly Simon album)")* (1990)[[31]](#cite_note-31)
* [Nina Simone](/wiki/Nina_Simone "Nina Simone") (whose 1958 debut album *[Little Girl Blue](/wiki/Little_Girl_Blue_(album) "Little Girl Blue (album)")* was named after the song)[[32]](#cite_note-32)
* [Frank Sinatra](/wiki/Frank_Sinatra "Frank Sinatra") – *[Songs for Young Lovers](/wiki/Songs_for_Young_Lovers "Songs for Young Lovers")* (1954)[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* [Sarah Vaughan](/wiki/Sarah_Vaughan "Sarah Vaughan") – *[Sarah Vaughan Sings Broadway: Great Songs from Hit Shows](/wiki/Sarah_Vaughan_Sings_Broadway:_Great_Songs_from_Hit_Shows "Sarah Vaughan Sings Broadway: Great Songs from Hit Shows")* (1958)[[34]](#cite_note-34)
* [Margaret Whiting](/wiki/Margaret_Whiting "Margaret Whiting") – this charted briefly in 1947[[35]](#cite_note-35)
* [Nancy Wilson](/wiki/Nancy_Wilson_(jazz_singer) "Nancy Wilson (jazz singer)") - *[Hello Young Lovers](/wiki/Hello_Young_Lovers_(Nancy_Wilson_album) "Hello Young Lovers (Nancy Wilson album)")* (1962)[[36]](#cite_note-36)
* [Pinky Winters](/wiki/Pinky_Winters "Pinky Winters") – *Pinky* (1954)
* [Louis Armstrong](/wiki/Louis_Armstrong "Louis Armstrong") – *Pops Is Tops-The Verve Studio Albums* (2018)[[37]](#cite_note-37)
* [Laura Mvula](/wiki/Laura_Mvula "Laura Mvula") – *[Music from and Inspired by 12 Years a Slave](/wiki/12_Years_a_Slave_(soundtrack) "12 Years a Slave (soundtrack)")* (2013)
